subject: [PATCH] x86/entry: Read CR2 in asm entry stub to redcue NMI clobbering window

reviews.author: Dave Hansen
reviews.summary: Reviewer Dave Hansen questioned whether the added assembly code is worth it to reduce but not eliminate a race condition for non-FRED systems, asking about the actual impact on users.
reviews.raw_body:
I'm all for using the FRED data. That's a no-brainer.

But I'm not sure the assembly and plumbing is worth it just to _reduce_
but not eliminate a race for non-FRED systems.

What's the actual end-user-visible fallout from the race? Don't we just
see the KASAN fault, skip it, IRET from the #PF and repeat the #PF?

reviews.author: Rik Riel (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledges a potential issue in production systems due to an NMI/CR2 race window, but lacks information on symptoms and frequency.
reviews.raw_body:
That's a good question.

I have only "seen" this in syzkaller, so I don't know
what the symptoms would look like if a regular system
hit this race window.

I would not be surprised if we occasionally hit this
in production, but given that I don't know what the
symptoms would look like, I have no idea how often.


-- 
All Rights Reversed.

reviews.author: Dave Hansen
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Dave Hansen noted that the idtentry_body_pf macro does not handle the case where the error_entry function is already using the %rdx register, which could lead to a register clobbering issue.

Reviewer Dave Hansen expressed concern that the patch does not provide a robust solution for the problem of NMI clobbering CR2, and suggested focusing on implementing the FRED bits instead.
reviews.raw_body:
On 5/13/26 09:43, Rik van Riel wrote:
...

---

I don't think there's a concrete enough problem being solved here to
justify adding complexity to the entry code. Especially since it's not
even a robust solution for the problem.

The FRED thing is simple, robust, and doesn't affect the actual (nasty)
entry assembly.

I think we should do the FRED bits and continue to ignore the pre-FRED
problem.

2026-05-13.patch_summary: This patch reduces the window where an NMI can clobber the CR2 register by reading it immediately after error_entry in the asm entry stub, rather than in the C handler. This is done by moving the read_cr2() function into the asm entry stub and passing the address as a third argument to exc_page_fault(). The patch also updates various files to use the new idtentry_pf macro and passes the CR2 value correctly to the C handler. This change reduces the NMI/CR2 race window by 90%.

reviews.author: Andrew Cooper
reviews.summary: The reviewer noted that CR2 is not the only register affected by the NMI/CR2 race window, and that DR6 and XFD_ERR are also problematic. They emphasized that preserving these registers requires the NMI handler to do so in assembly before entering C.
reviews.raw_body:
Well, corrupting CR2 is a real problem.

The very best case is that fixing the pagefault at the wrong CR2 is
benign, then rerun the faulting instruction having not fixed it up, and
get a repeat fault which this time wins the race with the NMI and
doesn't corrupt CR2, and gets fixed up properly.

It's not just CR2 which is a problem; DR6 and XFD_ERR. (the other
registers which have automatic side effects from exception delivery).

Really, the NMI handler needs to preserve the registers it potentially
clobbers, and that still requires the NMI handler doing it in assembly
before entering C.

~Andrew

reviews.author: Dave Hansen
reviews.summary: Reviewer Dave Hansen expressed agreement and a lack of enthusiasm for the patch's solution, noting that it is not complex but rather something they've 'lived with forever'.
reviews.raw_body:
Yup, totally agree.

It's not rocket science, and it's what we do for CR3 because of PTI, but
it's also no fun. So I didn't want to suggest it.

I mean, we've lived with this behavior _forever_, right? So how bad
could it be?

2026-05-14.patch_summary: This patch reduces the window where an NMI can clobber the CR2 register by reading it immediately after error_entry in the asm entry stub, rather than in the C handler. This is done by moving the read_cr2() function into the asm entry stub and passing the address as a third argument to exc_page_fault(). The patch also updates various files to use the new idtentry_pf macro and passes the CR2 value correctly to the C handler. This change reduces the NMI/CR2 race window by 90%.
